This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n4\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n5\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Overview\n8\t//!\n9\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n10\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n11\t//!\n12\t//! ```yaml\n13\t//! services:\n14\t//!   web:\n15\t//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n16\t//!     port: 8080\n17\t//!   database:\n18\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n19\t//!     port: 5432\n20\t//! ```\n21\t//!\n22\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\n23\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n24\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n25\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n26\t//!\n27\t//! # Architecture\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ## Scope\n30\t//!\n31\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n32\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n33\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n34\t//! - Line number where this scope started\n35\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n36\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n37\t//!\n38\t//! ## ScopeStack\n39\t//!\n40\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n41\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n42\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n43\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n44\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n45\t//!\n46\t//! # Examples\n47\t//!\n48\t//! ```\n49\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n50\t//!"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1f2c9d52-ccc5-419b-be68-9db45abe849f","uuid":"bf863313-4410-4f4a-bdb4-90555c2069f2","timestamp":"2026-07-13T19:54:11.152Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs","content":"//! Scope representation data structures\n//!\n//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n//!\n//! # Overview\n//!\n//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n//!\n//! ```yaml\n//! services:\n//!   web:\n//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n//!     port: 8080\n//!   database:\n//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n//!     port: 5432\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The scope representation system tracks:\n//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n//!\n//! # Architecture\n//!\n//! ## Scope\n//!\n//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n//! - Line number where this scope started\n//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n//!\n//! ## ScopeStack\n//!\n//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n//!\n//! # Examples\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n//!","numLines":50,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1163}}}

2);\n46\t\n47\t    // Enter second sequence item at same indent\n48\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n49\t\n50\t    // Keys should be cleared for new item\n51\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n52\t    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"name\"));\n53\t    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"value\"));\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t#[test]\n57\tfn test_sequence_scope_entry_increments_item_id() {\n58\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n59\t\n60\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n61\t    let id1 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n62\t\n63\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 3);\n64\t    let id2 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n65\t\n66\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n67\t    let id3 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n68\t\n69\t    assert_eq!(id1, Some(1));\n70\t    assert_eq!(id2, Some(2));\n71\t    assert_eq!(id3, Some(3));\n72\t}\n73\t\n74\t#[test]\n75\tfn test_sequence_scope_entry_creates_isolated_scope() {\n76\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n77\t\n78\t    // First sequence item\n79\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n80\t    stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).unwrap();\n81\t    stack.add_key(\"port\", 3).unwrap();\n82\t\n83\t    // Second sequence item at same indent\n84\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n85\t\n86\t    // Should be able to add same keys without duplicate error\n87\t    stack.add_key(\"host\", 6).unwrap();\n88\t    stack.add_key(\"port\", 7).unwrap();\n89\t\n90\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 2);\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\t// =============================================================================\n94\t// Sequence Scope Exit Behavior\n95\t// =============================================================================\n96\t\n97\t#[test]\n98\tfn test_sequence_scope_exit_updates_scope_state() {\n99\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n100\t\n101\t    // Create parent mapping scope\n102\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n103\t\n104\t    // Enter sequence scope\n105\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n106\t    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n107\t    stack.add_key(\"name\", 3).unwrap();\n108\t\n109\t    // Exit from sequence back to parent mapping\n110\t assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"items\");\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t#[test]\n119\tfn test_sequence_scope_exit_clears_sequence_keys() {\n120\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n121\t\n122\t    // Parent scope\n123\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n124\t    stack.add_key(\"parent_key\", 2).unwrap();\n125\t\n126\t    // Sequence scope\n127\t exist\n134\t    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"parent_key\"));\n135\t    // Sequence key should be gone\n136\t    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"seq_key\"));\n137\t}\n138\t\n139\t#[test]\n140\tfn test_sequence_scope_exit_to_different_indents() {\n141\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n142\t\n143\t    // Create nested structure: level1 (2) -> sequence (4) -> level3 (6)\n144\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n145\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n146\t    stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n147\t\n148\t    // Exit from level3 to level1 (skipping sequence)\n149\t    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n150\t\n151\t    // Should be at level1 scope\n152\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n153\t    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1\");\n154\t    assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n155\t}\n156\t\n157\t// Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n341\t\n342\t    // Sequence in mapping\n343\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n344\t    stack.add_key(\"name\", 3).unwrap();\n345\t\n346\t    // Mapping in {\n413\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n414\t\n415\t    // Simulate:\n416\t    // application:\n417\t    //   services:\n418\t    //     - name: web\n419\t    //       config:\n420\t    //         port: 8080\n421\t    //         host: localhost\n422\t    //     - name: database\n423\t    //       config:\n424\t    //         port: 5432\n425\t    //         host: db.example.com\n426\t\n427\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"application\".to_string()));\n428\t    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n429\t\n430\t    // First service item\n431\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(6, 3);\n432\t    stack.add_key(\"name\", 4).unwrap();\n433\t    stack.enter_scope(8, 5, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n434\t    stack.add_key(\"port\", 6).unwrap();\n435\t    stack.add_key(\"host\", 7).unwrap();\n436\t\n437\t    // Verify first service\n438\t    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"application.services.config\");\n439\t    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"port\"));\n440\t    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n441\t\n442\t    // Exit to services level for second item\n443\t    stack.exit_to_scope(4);\n444\t\n445\t    // Second service item\n446\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(6, 9);\n447\t    stack.add_key(\"name\", 10).unwrap();\n448\t    stack.enter_scope(8, 11, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n449\t\n450\t    // Same keys should be OK\n451\t    stack.add_key(\"port\", 12).unwrap();\n452\t    stack.add_key(\"host\", 13).unwrap();\n453\t\n454\t    // Verify second service has same keys\n455\t    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"port\"));\n456\t    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n457\t}\n458\t\n459\t// =============================================================================\n460\t// Comprehensive verification tests for sequence scope handling\n//!\n//! These tests verify that sequence scope tracking works correctly in YAML parsing,\n//! especially for nested sequences and mixed mappings.\n//!\n//! Bead: bf-1ccile\n//!\n//! Test Coverage:\n//! - Sequence scope entry is tracked correctly\n//! - Sequence scope exit maintains proper scope state\n//! - Nested sequence/mapping combinations work\n//! - Test coverage demonstrates correctness\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::{ScopeStack};\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::parser::BasicParser;\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::parser::Parser;\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Sequence Scope Entry Tracking\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_entry_sets_in_sequence_context() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Before entry: should not be in sequence context\n    assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n\n    // Enter sequence scope\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n\n    // After entry: should be in sequence context\n    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_entry_clears_previous_keys() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Add keys to first sequence item\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"value\", 3).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 2);\n\n    // Enter second sequence item at same indent\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n\n    // Keys should be cleared for new item\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"name\"));\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"value\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_entry_increments_item_id() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    let id1 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 3);\n    let id2 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n    let id3 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n    assert_eq!(id1, Some(1));\n    assert_eq!(id2, Some(2));\n    assert_eq!(id3, Some(3));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_entry_creates_isolated_scope() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // First sequence item\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"port\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    // Second sequence item at same indent\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n\n    // Should be able to add same keys without duplicate error\n    stack.add_key(\"host\", 6).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"port\", 7).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 2);\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Sequence Scope Exit Behavior\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_exit_updates_scope_state() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Create parent mapping scope\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n\n    // Enter sequence scope\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    // Exit from sequence back to parent mapping\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n    // Should be back at items scope\n    assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"items\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_exit_clears_sequence_keys() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Parent scope\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"parent_key\", 2).unwrap();\n\n    // Sequence scope\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 3);\n    stack.add_key(\"seq_key\", 4).unwrap();\n\n    // Exit from sequence\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n    // Parent key should still exist\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"parent_key\"));\n    // Sequence key should be gone\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"seq_key\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_exit_to_different_indents() {\n stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n    stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n\n    // Exit from level3 to level1 (skipping sequence)\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n    // Should be at level1 scope\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1\");\n    assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n}\n\n// test_sequence_mapping_sequence_pattern() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Pattern: mapping -> sequence -> mapping -> sequence\n\n    // Root mapping\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n    // Sequence in mapping\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    // Mapping in sequence\n    stack.enter_scope(6, test_alternating_sequence_and_mapping() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Sequence\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    stack.add_key(\"seq1_key\", 2).unwrap();\n\n    // 2);\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1\");\n    assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_multiple_consecutive_sequence_entries_same_indent() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Multiple sequence entries at same indent without keys\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 2);\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 3);\n\n    // Should have incremented item IDs each time\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, Some(3));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_entry_preserves_parent_scopes() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Create parent scopes\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent1\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"parent2\".to_string()));\n\n    // Add key to parent2\n    stack.add_key(\"parent_key\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    // Enter sequence scope\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(6, 4);\n\n    // Parent scopes should still be in stack\n    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 4); // root + parent1 + parent2 + sequence\n\n    // Exit sequence\n    stack.exit_to_scope(4);\n\n    // Parent key should still exist\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"parent_key\"));\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent1.parent2\");\n}\n","numLines":724,"startLine":1,"totalLines":724}}}
